receipts.analyst_basis: Confirmed by inspection alone: both generic prompts say 'the person' while REL and WORK vignettes each contain two eligible referents and no binding rule exists in the instrument; the scripted RS-PERSPECTIVE-QUESTION response is a legal, unmappable reading. Source: author-walkthrough-v0 WT-001 (prompt_catalog/0 and /1).

Confirmed by inspection alone: REL stimulus introduces 'The speaker' and every REL context bullet says 'this partner'; the identity is required for comprehension and never stated. Source: language-comprehension-inspection-v0 LC-002 (rel-boundary.json stimulus vs bullets).

Confirmed by inspection alone: participant-facing descriptors say 'The vignette re-presents ...' (both REL cued-material levels) and 'the vignette states that ...' (RISK no-incident level); text delivered to a participant refers to itself as a vignette. Source: author-walkthrough-v0 WT-002.

Confirmed by inspection alone: FUTURE-REL probe options expose internal design vocabulary ('At a new access', 'prior-material cue', 'no new ... external target evidence') and restate the manipulated mood level at E2. The literal-leak scanner is silent on these because no registered identifier appears; the leak is semantic. Sources: WT-003, LC-006 (trace-oracle-v1.json probe_options).

Confirmed by inspection alone: context lines render construct labels as self-reports ('reports low evaluation threat', 'reports high bodily arousal', 'reports an ordinary current mood'); people do not report in construct vocabulary. Source: WT-004 (all three family mood/arousal/threat factor levels).

Confirmed by inspection alone: within one participant surface, the stimulus line is natural speech while the three bullets are compiled research-register descriptors; the register seam marks which sentences carry manipulated content. Source: LC-004.

Confirmed by inspection alone: the RISK equality bullet packs number/timing/duration/loudness plus a two-turn condition into one sentence with a referent-free 'the same', and RISK bullets are systematically heavier than REL/WORK bullets, so family difficulty is unmatched. Sources: WT-005, LC-003.

Confirmed by inspection alone: 'the incident was independently resolved as following' uses project-internal 'resolved as following'; a lay reader cannot parse it as intended. Source: LC-001 (risk-footsteps.json history level 1).

Confirmed mechanically: P1 preregistration section 10 registers mapping statuses including NOT_APPLIED and NOT_EVALUABLE, but the frozen development-assessment schema enum has no counterpart, and absent-payload events (REFUSED / NO_RESPONSE / TECHNICAL_FAILURE) admit no mapping status at all; the P1 mapping candidate sets therefore silently skip them. Source: LC-005; observable in every P1 mapping candidate set for runs containing absent-payload scripts.

Deferred: the fixed three-bullet declared-factor surface is a frozen 001A design decision, and whether it anchors responses is an acquisition-side question that scripted replay cannot answer. Source: WT-006.

Deferred: whether fusing 'say or do' into one slot loses information can only be judged against actual responses; the scripted null-action case shows the ambiguity is expressible, not that it occurs. Source: WT-007.

Deferred: the missing time-scale anchor for 'first'/'next' and for 'At a new access' is real on inspection, but whether respondents actually diverge in horizon requires acquisition data; a wording fix without evidence risks a premature freeze. Sources: WT-008, LC-007.

Deferred: D0's tendency to elicit constructed explanations is demonstrated as expressible by the scripted corpus (RS-D0-NEW-EXPLANATION) but its occurrence rate is an acquisition question; the instrument already denies D0 any oracle coordinate. Source: WT-009.

Rejected: on wording inspection the REL aggregate-history factor and the cued-single-episode factor remain distinct (aggregate 'across comparable prior boundary requests' vs single 're-presents a prior occasion'); the surface overlap does not merge attributions. Source: WT-010.
